title: Fillman Platform Headboard
summary: A plywood-panel headboard with a mitered inset-moulding square as its centerpiece, built as its own leg-and-header frame rather than an added-on bed accessory.
description:
The plywood panel is the structural core here — pocket holes drilled around its back edges are what the legs and header both attach to — with panel trim nailed to the front face first, before any of the frame goes on. **Clamp the trim in place and nail from the back** rather than face-nailing, which hides every nail hole and avoids the pocket hole locations at the same time.

Attach the legs flush to the top of the panel trim, matching their thickness to the panel-plus-trim combined, then attach the header flush to the back with even overhangs on both ends. Once the frame is together, attach the panel to the legs and the base to the frame with pocket hole screws sized to the material thickness at each joint — 1-1/4" screws for the 3/4" panel, 2-1/2" for the thicker base.

**The mitered moulding square is the finishing detail that makes this headboard, not an afterthought** — cut and carefully position the four moulding pieces last, measuring and marking their placement rather than eyeballing it, since a slightly off-center inset square is the kind of flaw that draws the eye on an otherwise plain panel.

materials.name: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

cutList.note: Long point to long point, both ends mitered at 45°, ends not parallel.
Long point to long point, both ends mitered at 45°, ends not parallel.

steps.title: Drill the panel pocket holes
steps.body: Drill 3/4" pocket holes around the side and top edges of the panel's back side, for attaching the legs and header in later steps.

steps.title: Attach the panel trim
steps.body: On the front side of the panel, attach the trim with 1-1/4" finish nails and glue, clamping in place and nailing from the back to hide nail holes.

steps.title: Add the legs
steps.body: Attach the legs with glue and 1-1/4" pocket hole screws, flush to the top of the panel trim.

steps.title: Attach the header
steps.body: Attach the header flush to the back with even overhangs on both ends, using 1-1/4" pocket holes and wood glue.

steps.title: Attach the panel and base
steps.body: Attach the panel to the legs with 1-1/4" pocket hole screws. Attach the base with 2-1/2" pocket hole screws.

steps.title: Add the moulding
steps.body: Cut the moulding pieces and carefully position and attach with glue and 3/4" finish nails.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Sand all surfaces and apply primer and paint, or stain and poly.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

images.alt: Fillman platform headboard with inset moulding square
